name: Podoconiosis (Non-Filarial Elephantiasis)
description: A geobotanical inflammatory lymphadenopathy triggered by chronic barefeet exposure to alkaline volcanic red clay soils. The soil micro-particles penetrate skin and cause progressive, severe lymphatic blockage and swelling.

climateLinks.impact: Barefoot farming in highland red soils exposes subsistence workers to toxic mineral micro-silicates that initiate dermal lymph vessel inflammation.

prevention: Wearing thick leather shoes or boots consistently
Rigorous daily washing of feet with clean water and acidic antiseptic soap
Laying concrete floor slabs in rural houses
triageAdvisory: Clinical features: Bilateral, asymmetric swelling of lower legs. Rule out Wuchereria bancrofti filariasis first. Triage is centered on leg hygiene: wash daily, apply petroleum jelly, and elevate feet at night.
